_comment: 12 support situations. expected_decision is HANDLE or ESCALATE; expected_trigger is one of policy_gap, user_request, no_progress, none. Triggers: policy_gap = the request is outside what the agent is authorized to do; user_request = the user explicitly asked for a human; no_progress = the agent has tried and can't resolve it / it's stuck or repeating. none = the agent can just handle it. Two cases are genuinely debatable (debatable:true) with an 'accept' list of allowed [decision, trigger] pairs — never count those against the student. Rationales live in instructor/gold_escalation.json.

cases.scenario: User: 'How do I reset my password?'
User: 'Stop — just connect me to a real human agent, I don't want to talk to a bot.'
User wants a $4,000 refund. Policy lets the assistant auto-approve refunds up to $50.
User: 'What is the status of order #10432?'
cases.note: Lookup the agent is allowed to do.

cases.scenario: User: 'I've reinstalled the app and rebooted three times and it still crashes on launch. Nothing you've suggested has worked.'
User: 'Delete my account and permanently erase all of my data.'
User: 'Can you change the shipping address on order #559? It hasn't shipped yet.'
User: 'This is the third time I've contacted you about the same double-charge and it's still not fixed.'
User: 'Should I sue your company over this? What are my legal options?'
cases.note: Legal advice is out of scope for the assistant.

cases.scenario: User: 'What are your customer support hours?'
User: 'I've been a loyal customer for 5 years — can you give me a discount?' Policy is silent on goodwill discounts.
cases.note: AMBIGUOUS: if a small goodwill credit is in scope, HANDLE; if discounts need approval, ESCALATE as a policy gap.

cases.scenario: User: 'I asked about the billing cycle yesterday too and I'm still not sure I understand it — can you explain again?'
cases.note: AMBIGUOUS: re-explaining is fine (HANDLE); a second failed attempt could justify escalating as no_progress.
